codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the disk-backed ground truth labeling system. The code correctly adapts Mode 1's ground-truth.js localStorage pattern to disk persistence via serve.py POST endpoints. All 8 feature requirements are covered: VGTRingPrimitive with 3-class colored rings, click-to-label popover with viewport clamping, POST to /api/labels/{week} with correct label object schema, fetch from /data/labels/{week}.json with 404→empty handling, export via Blob URL, lock panel with nested param rendering, and persistence across refresh and week switching. The code follows project conventions (underscore-prefixed privates, init guard, vanilla JS, design tokens). Integration with validate-chart.js and validate-app.js is clean with proper typeof guards for loose coupling.

issues.description: saveVGTLabels() is called without await in setVGTLabel(). If a user labels two detections in rapid succession, two concurrent POSTs could race. However, since the _vgtLabels array is mutated synchronously before each POST and the full array is sent, the last-writer-wins behavior is safe — the server writes atomically. Still, adding await would be slightly more robust.

resetValidateGT() is defined but never called from any code path. The chart is created once (guarded by _vChartCreated flag in validate-chart.js) and never recreated, so this function is dead code. Not harmful, but could be removed or wired up if chart recreation is ever needed.

In _renderParamRows(), param keys and values from locked.json are inserted into innerHTML without HTML entity escaping. If any param key or value contains <, >, or & characters, it could break the HTML rendering. Since the source is the app's own locked.json (not user input), the risk is very low.

Save failures (e.g., server down) are only logged via console.warn with no visual feedback to the user. User might think a label was saved when it wasn't. A toast notification pattern could improve UX but is not required by the feature spec.

loadWeekData() fetches vApp.labelsData from disk, but validate-gt.js maintains its own _vgtLabels state via loadVGTLabels(). The vApp.labelsData loaded by validate-app.js is never consumed by any code. Minor redundancy — the extra fetch is harmless but wastes one HTTP request per week switch.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validate-gt.js file is 681 lines (handoff says ~470 lines). The diff adds 957 lines total across 4 files (681 JS + 240 HTML/CSS + 27 app + 9 chart). AGENTS.md doesn't specify a file size guideline, but this is a notable size for a single JS module — worth noting if future features grow similarly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 379faa4 --stat shows validate-gt.js at 681 insertions, handoff says 470 lines.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly used typeof guards (e.g., `if (typeof initValidateGT === 'function')`) for cross-file function calls, consistent with the loosely-coupled vanilla JS architecture. This pattern is not explicitly documented in AGENTS.md but is a good practice that should be maintained.

summary: Feature passes review. The ground-truth disk labeling system is well-implemented, correctly adapting Mode 1's localStorage pattern to disk persistence via serve.py. All 8 feature requirements are fully covered: colored ring primitive, click-to-label popover, POST on label, fetch on load, export, lock panel, refresh persistence, and week-switch persistence. Five non-blocking issues identified: unawaited save call (safe due to last-writer-wins), dead resetValidateGT function, unescaped innerHTML for params, silent save failures, and redundant label fetch in validate-app.js. None are blocking — the feature works correctly as designed.
